questions.text: The bouncing back of light into the same medium is called:
questions.explanation: Reflection follows laws like angle of incidence equals angle of reflection.

questions.text: Mirrors which have reflecting surface behind the glass are:
questions.explanation: In plane mirrors, silvering is done at the back to protect the reflecting surface.

questions.text: The image formed by a plane mirror cannot be:
options: Formed at same distance behind mirror
questions.explanation: Lateral inversion means left appears right and top appears bottom.

questions.text: Spherical mirrors are parts of a:
questions.explanation: Concave and convex mirrors are silvered parts of a hollow sphere.

questions.text: The reflecting surface of a concave mirror curves:
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors converge light rays to a point.

questions.text: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles because they:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors give a wider view, useful for safety while driving.

questions.text: The point on the principal axis where parallel rays converge after reflection from concave mirror is:
questions.explanation: Focus or focal point of concave mirror.

questions.text: Focal length of a spherical mirror is half of its:
questions.explanation: F = R/2, where R is radius of curvature.

questions.text: Object placed between pole and focus of concave mirror forms:
questions.explanation: Such images are used in shaving mirrors.

questions.text: In concave mirrors, dentist uses it for clear image of teeth because it forms:
questions.explanation: Magnified erect image when object is between focus and pole.

questions.text: The change in direction of light passing from one transparent medium to another is called:
questions.explanation: Refraction occurs due to change in speed of light in different media.

questions.text: Light bends ________ water.
questions.explanation: Light travels slower in water (denser medium), so bends towards normal.

questions.text: The angle between incident ray and normal is:
questions.explanation: Normal is perpendicular to surface at point of incidence.

questions.text: A prism splits white light into:
questions.explanation: VIBGYOR spectrum due to dispersion.

questions.text: The human eye forms image on:
questions.explanation: Retina contains light-sensitive cells that send signals to brain.

questions.text: Far point of normal human eye is:
questions.explanation: Normal eye can see distant objects clearly up to infinity.

questions.text: Near point of normal eye is:
questions.explanation: Least distance of distinct vision is 25 cm.

questions.text: Myopia (short-sightedness) is corrected using:
questions.explanation: Concave lens diverges rays so image forms on retina.

questions.text: Hypermetropia (long-sightedness) is corrected using:
questions.explanation: Convex lens converges rays for nearby objects.

questions.text: The black dot/pigment behind cornea is:
questions.explanation: Iris controls amount of light entering through pupil.

questions.text: Regular reflection produces:
questions.explanation: Like mirror image.

questions.text: Laws of reflection are applicable to:
questions.explanation: Hold for all polished surfaces.

questions.text: Image in convex mirror is always:
questions.explanation: Useful as rear view mirrors.

questions.text: When object is at centre of curvature of concave mirror, image is:
questions.explanation: Image at same position as object.

questions.text: Pencil partially in water appears bent due to:
questions.explanation: Apparent shift due to refraction.

questions.text: Twinkling of stars is due to:
questions.explanation: Light bends multiple times in varying air density.

questions.text: Danger signs on roads are triangular and red because:
questions.explanation: Red penetrates fog/dust better.

questions.text: Power of accommodation means:
questions.explanation: Eye lens changes shape via ciliary muscles.

questions.text: Presbyopia occurs due to:
questions.explanation: Common in old age; corrected by bifocal lenses.

questions.text: Kaleidoscope works on principle of:
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors at angles produce patterns.

questions.text: Periscope uses:
questions.explanation: To see over obstacles.

questions.text: In solar cookers, concave mirrors are used to:
questions.explanation: Concentrate heat at focal point.

questions.text: White light splits into colours by glass prism due to:
questions.explanation: Violet bends most, red least.

questions.text: Blind spot in eye is where:
questions.explanation: No light-sensitive cells there.

questions.text: Aman sees nearby objects clearly but distant ones blurred. He has:
questions.explanation: Image forms before retina.

questions.text: Lightning conductors are painted white because:
questions.explanation: Stay cool, effective protection.

questions.text: Mirage in desert is due to:
questions.explanation: Hot air near ground bends light.

questions.text: Which mirror is used in car headlights?
questions.explanation: Parallel beam from focal point.

questions.text: Rainbow formation involves:
options: Refraction, dispersion, total internal reflection, refraction
questions.explanation: In water droplets.

questions.text: The part of eye providing most refraction is:
questions.explanation: Cornea provides 2/3 of total refraction.
